title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Mercimek Corbasi: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup. This easy, comforting recipe is packed with flavor and perfect for any meal.
seo.h1: Authentic Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)
h2Headings: Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Frequently Asked Questions about Mercimek Corbasi
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of traditional Mercimek Corbasi, Turkish Red Lentil Soup, garnished with fresh herbs and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a comforting bowl of Mercimek Corbasi, the classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a cornerstone of Turkish cuisine, a comforting and deeply flavorful red lentil soup. This creamy, vibrant soup is incredibly easy to prepare, offering warmth and nourishment in every spoonful. Perfect as a starter or a light meal, it embodies the simple elegance of Turkish home cooking.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables and Lentils
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well. Chop the onion and carrot into small, even pieces.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Liquids
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Add the rinsed red lentils, 6 cups of water or vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have completely broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom.

instructions.title: Blend for Creaminess
instructions.text: Remove the pot from heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Topping (Sos)
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter (or 1 tablespoon of olive oil for a vegan option) over medium-low heat. Add the dried mint and pul biber. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual bowls. Drizzle each serving with a spoonful of the hot spiced butter and mint topping. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, as lemon brightens the flavors beautifully.

tips: For an extra smooth soup, you can pass it through a fine-mesh sieve after blending, though it's usually not necessary with a good immersion blender.
Adjust the amount of pul biber to your preference; Turkish chili flakes offer a mild, fruity heat. If you prefer more spice, you can add a pinch more or use a spicier variety.
Mercimek Corbasi tastes even better the next day. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. It reheats well on the stovetop or in the microwave.
article:
A culinary journey through Turkey is incomplete without experiencing its rich tapestry of soups and stews, and among the most beloved is Mercimek Corbasi. This vibrant, comforting red lentil soup holds a special place in the heart of Turkish cuisine, gracing tables from bustling city restaurants to humble village homes. It's a dish that embodies warmth, simplicity, and profound flavor, offering a nourishing embrace in every spoonful. Whether you're seeking a quick, healthy meal or a taste of authentic Turkish tradition, Mercimek Corbasi is a perfect choice. This article will guide you through everything you need to know about this iconic soup, from its ingredients and preparation to serving suggestions and expert tips, ensuring you can recreate its magic in your own kitchen.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-MEK chor-ba-suh," literally translates to "lentil soup." It is a staple Turkish soup renowned for its silky smooth texture, beautiful reddish-orange hue, and deeply savory flavor profile. Unlike many lentil soups that might be chunky, Mercimek Corbasi is traditionally pureed until incredibly creamy, despite containing no actual cream. Its richness comes from the simple combination of red lentils, aromatic vegetables like onions and garlic, and a touch of tomato paste, all simmered together to perfection.

This soup is more than just a dish; it's a cultural icon in Turkey. It’s a common breakfast item, a comforting lunch, and a satisfying starter for dinner. You'll find it served in almost every lokanta (local eatery) and restaurant across the country. Its popularity stems not only from its delicious taste but also from its nutritional value. Red lentils are packed with protein, fiber, and essential minerals, making Mercimek Corbasi a wholesome and incredibly filling meal that's naturally gluten-free and vegan-friendly. The soup is often finished with a drizzle of "nane yağı" – a vibrant, sizzling butter infused with dried mint and red pepper flakes (pul biber), which adds an irresistible aromatic and slightly spicy kick.

## Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its simple, readily available ingredients that, when combined, create a complex and satisfying flavor. Here’s what you’ll need:

*   **Red Lentils (split):** The star of the show. Split red lentils cook quickly and break down easily, contributing to the soup's characteristic smooth texture.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. Sautéing these until soft and fragrant builds the essential flavor base.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** A crucial ingredient for depth of flavor and color. Turkish tomato paste is often richer and more concentrated than Western varieties.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** For sautéing the aromatics and creating the flavorful chili-mint butter drizzle.
*   **Water or Broth:** While water is traditionally used, vegetable or chicken broth can add extra richness.
*   **Dried Mint (Kuru Nane):** Essential for the authentic Turkish flavor. It’s used both in the soup and in the finishing butter.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber):** Adds a gentle warmth and color. Adjust to your spice preference.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season the soup to perfection.
*   **Optional Additions:** A small carrot or potato can be added for extra body and sweetness, though many traditional recipes keep it simpler. A pinch of flour can be used for thickening, but thorough blending usually makes this unnecessary.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** Absolutely essential for serving, adding a bright, tangy finish.

## How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step

Creating a delicious pot of Mercimek Corbasi is surprisingly straightforward. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl every time:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any impurities and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a generous drizzle of olive oil or a knob of butter over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until it becomes soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Build Flavor with Tomato Paste:** Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. This step is crucial as it deepens the flavor of the paste and removes any raw taste. If using, add chopped carrot or potato at this stage and sauté briefly.
4.  **Add Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot. Pour in the water or broth, ensuring the lentils are fully submerged. Bring the mixture to a boil.
5.  **Season and Simmer:** Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Add salt, black pepper, and half of the dried mint and red pepper flakes directly into the soup.
6.  **Blend to Perfection:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth. Return the blended soup to the pot.
7.  **Adjust Consistency and Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust salt and pepper if needed. Bring the soup back to a gentle simmer for a few more minutes.
8.  **Prepare the Chili-Mint Butter (Nane Yağı):** In a small pan, melt a tablespoon or two of butter over medium heat. Once melted and slightly foamy, remove from heat and stir in the remaining dried mint and red pepper flakes. The heat from the butter will bloom their flavors and aromas.
9.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the fragrant chili-mint butter over each serving. Offer fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing.

## Pro Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup

While the recipe for Mercimek Corbasi is straightforward, a few expert tips can elevate your soup from good to extraordinary:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils!** Don't skip rinsing. It makes a noticeable difference in the clarity and taste of your soup.
*   **Quality Tomato Paste:** Invest in good quality tomato paste, ideally Turkish "salça." Its concentrated flavor is key to the soup's authentic taste.
*   **Sautéing Time:** Allow sufficient time to sauté the onions until truly soft and the tomato paste to cook out its raw flavor. This builds the fundamental depth.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** The signature texture of Mercimek Corbasi is its creaminess. Use an immersion blender or a regular blender to get it as smooth as possible. Don't be afraid to blend for a few minutes.
*   **The Lemon Squeeze is Crucial:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice brightens all the flavors and adds a necessary tangy counterpoint to the richness of the lentils and butter. Don't serve without it!
*   **Don't Skimp on the Butter Drizzle:** The nane yağı (chili-mint butter) is not just a garnish; it's an integral part of the Mercimek Corbasi experience, adding aroma, flavor, and a beautiful visual touch.

## Serving Suggestions & Variations

Mercimek Corbasi is wonderfully versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways:

*   **Traditional Serving:** Always serve with fresh lemon wedges on the side. A generous squeeze of lemon juice stirred into the hot soup just before eating is non-negotiable for an authentic experience. It's also commonly enjoyed with a side of crusty bread (like pita or lavash) for dipping, and sometimes pickled vegetables (turşu).
*   **Spice Level:** The amount of pul biber can be adjusted to your preference. For more heat, add extra to the butter drizzle or directly into the soup. For a milder soup, reduce or omit it.
*   **Vegetable Boost:** While traditionally simple, you can add finely diced carrots, potatoes, or even bell peppers along with the onions for extra nutrients and flavor. They will soften and blend seamlessly into the soup.
*   **Broth Base:** For an even richer flavor, substitute vegetable or chicken broth for water. This is a great way to deepen the umami profile.
*   **Garnishes:** Beyond the chili-mint butter, a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or a few fresh mint leaves can add color and freshness.
*   **Heartier Meal:** Serve Mercimek Corbasi as a light lunch with a side salad, or as a starter to a larger Turkish meal featuring kebabs or vegetable dishes.

## Frequently Asked Questions about Mercimek Corbasi

**Q: Is Mercimek Corbasi spicy?**
A: Traditionally, Mercimek Corbasi is not overly spicy. The red pepper flakes (pul biber) in the butter drizzle provide a gentle warmth rather than intense heat. You can adjust the amount of pul biber to suit your preference, making it milder or spicier.

**Q: Can I make Mercimek Corbasi ahead of time?**
A: Absolutely! Like many soups, Mercimek Corbasi often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld. Store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or broth if it has thickened too much.

**Q: Can I freeze Mercimek Corbasi?**
A: Yes, Mercimek Corbasi freezes beautifully. Allow it to cool completely, then transfer it to freezer-safe containers or bags. It can be frozen for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and reheat gently, adjusting consistency as needed.

**Q: What kind of lentils should I use for Mercimek Corbasi?**
A: It is essential to use split red lentils for Mercimek Corbasi. They cook quickly and break down easily, creating the characteristic smooth and creamy texture of the soup. Other types of lentils (like green or brown) will not yield the same result.

**Q: Do I need to blend Mercimek Corbasi?**
A: For the traditional, silky smooth consistency of Mercimek Corbasi, blending is crucial. While some rustic versions might leave it slightly chunky, the signature Turkish style is definitely pureed until creamy. An immersion blender makes this process very easy.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it’s a taste of Turkish hospitality and comfort in a bowl. Its simplicity belies a depth of flavor that is both nourishing and incredibly satisfying. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a novice in the kitchen, making this iconic red lentil soup is a rewarding experience. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to be transported to the vibrant culinary landscape of Turkey with every warm, flavorful spoonful of your homemade Mercimek Corbasi.
